title: How did the First Ancestral Race come to be
display_title: How did the First Ancestral Race come to be
wikitext:
[]: How did the First Ancestral Race come to be?             Quote Post by Margerald » Wed Sep 19, 2012 6:56 pm I know that a planet of sentient beings, known as the First Ancestral Race, stored the souls of 7 of their people inside Lilith and Adam. They cradled them in the White and Black moons along with 5 other such beings with the intent to create life on other planets because theirs was dying. Adam crash landed onto a young Earth some 4.6 billion years ago. With him came its Spear of Longinus. It was to populate the earth with his spawn, the Angels. Though, this did not happen because another Seed of Life (the 7 beings with souls stored inside them) also landed on Earth. Lilith lost its Spear of Longinus, which was to be used if such a situation ever arose to prevent the spawn of two Seeds of Life from inhabiting the same world. Because Lilith did not have its spear, Adam was inactivated, and Lilith's spawn inhabited the Earth. I understand all that. What I don't know is why the First Ancestral Race's planet was dying, and how the FAR came to be. Was it created by God? Note: The reason I wrote a two paragraph post rather than just this simple question is because I want to confirm that my interpretations are correct. If I'm wrong about any of this, please tell me.

[]:             Quote Post by Der Kommissar » Wed Sep 19, 2012 7:30 pm I don't think there's any canon information on where the FAR came from themselves. As to 'God,' there doesn't seem to be one as we understand it in Evangelion. Or, if there is a god, he's an absentee landlord.

[]:             Quote Post by arkiel » Wed Sep 19, 2012 7:54 pm Yea, and there came to be in the great black emptiness before stellar ignition a race of ordered macroscopic organisms, that subsisted off gravity and the emptiness of space itself. They trod paths of magnetism and saw by dint, not of photons, but of cascades of elementary energy that was plentiful in those days. Their hearts were beating engines of what we now call dark energy. They built the earliest black holes to create a nexus for communal interaction. When it was obvious the universe was about to ignite and render their form of life obsolete and impractical, the FAR undertook a great and awesome work, to ensure the order of life would survive the chaos of that first stellar ignition. And yea, for the header at the top of the page did say: "Anno has no bloody idea. Fanwank something."

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Sep 19, 2012 8:42 pm Since they're the first lifeforms to exist in the known universe, they probably came about the same way we did in real life. Evolution.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Fri Sep 21, 2012 4:29 pm Maybe they were seeded by the "Zeroeth Ancestral Race."

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Sep 21, 2012 10:48 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Since they're the first lifeforms to exist in the known universe, they probably came about the same way we did in real life. Evolution.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Sep 23, 2012 2:57 am In many ways, the FAR can be seen as the first race of powerful gods, among the level as the gods of Greek or Norse mythology, and that with their existence they came to create human begins like ourselves, the Angels, and whatever races they may have created. Or the the FAR may have been some equally random form of intelligent lifeforms that eventually gained the technology (and understood it) for creating the Seeds of Life that came to be central to the Evangelion sub-plot. Not much else information exists explaining their existence other than that the fact that they existed and they created the Seeds. Their story alone could be an interesting work in itself.

[]:             Quote Post by Merkaba » Mon Sep 24, 2012 11:58 am AuraTwilight wrote: Since they're the first lifeforms to exist in the known universe, they probably came about the same way we did in real life. Evolution.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Sep 24, 2012 12:49 pm Merkaba wrote: If you believe consciousness is a physical construct of sorts, then it always has existed since the beginning of time

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Sep 24, 2012 12:50 pm Merkaba wrote: ... Except that still doesn't explain the origins of consciousness.

[]:             Quote Post by qu4d » Mon Sep 24, 2012 1:52 pm Merkaba wrote: If you believe it's metaphysical, well, then you have to believe in divination, which I doubt you believe is a possibility, due to your straight-forward "the way we did in real life. Evolution." Either way, saying evolution is what created the FAR doesn't make much sense, since they're really just manifestations of a dichotomized collective unconscious. "The same way we [are] in real life."

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Sep 24, 2012 3:24 pm qu4d wrote: If there would be an answer to how did the FAR begin to exist with... you could go on and question this answer. That makes any discussion pointless.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Sep 24, 2012 3:51 pm Merkaba wrote: ... Except that still doesn't explain the origins of consciousness. If you believe consciousness is a physical construct of sorts, then it always has existed since the beginning of time, being that matter is neither created nor destroyed. But I doubt you believe in consciousness always existing, since you implied we (and the FAR) came about at a certain point in evolution. If you believe it's metaphysical, well, then you have to believe in divination, which I doubt you believe is a possibility, due to your straight-forward "the way we did in real life. Evolution." Either way, saying evolution is what created the FAR doesn't make much sense, since they're really just manifestations of a dichotomized collective unconscious. "The same way we [are] in real life."

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon Sep 24, 2012 6:37 pm The "souls" in question could easily be some sort of particle-wave duality information storage gobbledegook and only "metaphysical" because we are not sufficiently sophisticated to understand them.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Mon Sep 24, 2012 6:42 pm Chuckman wrote: only "metaphysical" because we are not sufficiently sophisticated to understand them.

[]:             Quote Post by Merkaba » Tue Sep 25, 2012 10:28 am AuraTwilight wrote: This is the dumbest thing I've ever read. If we replace the word "consciousness" with "internet" or "television", then it reveals everything wrong with what you just said. Regardless, none of it matters since souls exist in the Evaverse and can be scientifically tampered with. The existence of souls do not necessarily require the existence of divinity, either. That's just a fallacy.

[]:             Quote Post by Quadhonk » Tue Sep 25, 2012 10:44 am Merkaba wrote: Where's my gold star for coming up with the "dumbest thing you have ever read?" ... Though I guess I'm missing the part where what I said was dumb. Consciousness exists. Everything that exists exists within consciousness. Consciousness has always existed. it didn't just come out of nowhere. Everything that has ever existed began, if things indeed "began", with consciousness. How can you speculate otherwise? And try not to be a jackass when responding to someone's post. You calling something "dumb" and then advocating evolution is quite hypocritical. You have no more proof that evolution is real than you do religion, or anything else for that matter. You have no way of proving, without the shadow of a doubt, that your senses are the truth. You have no proof that what you're experiencing is real. You have no way of knowing that the people you interact with are nothing more than figments contained within your consciousness, which you believe possess consciousness as well. The only thing you can know, and there's a difference between knowing and believing, is that you exist, if indeed you exist. You believe evolution is real, that others exist, etc. but you only know that you exist and are experiencing something, if in fact are exist and are experiencing something like I am. I'll never know for sure. Just like you'll never know if I or the others on here or the people you interact with in "real life" exist as you do. So before you call people's thoughts dumb, stop and realize that the only truth is one's own existence of consciousness, and that everything else is belief. All beliefs are equally invalid, since you will never in your whole life be able to know anything with absolute certainty. Evolution isn't the exception to that.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Sep 25, 2012 10:46 am Merkaba wrote: Consciousness exists. Everything that exists exists within consciousness . Consciousness has always existed. it didn't just come out of nowhere. Everything that has ever existed began, if things indeed "began", with consciousness . How can you speculate otherwise?

[]:             Quote Post by Merkaba » Tue Sep 25, 2012 10:56 am I think it means what I think it means. Perhaps you have the wrong idea of what it means. We'll never be able to know for sure. You are consciousness. That's what I mean. Reality exists within your perception of it, and there's no way of saying that it exists outside/independently of you/your perception, since you'll never be able to experience it otherwise to know for sure. It's just pure speculation on your part, which was my point. EDIT: And don't be mistaken - I'm not misusing "consciousness" in place of "conscious." I'm literally saying that you and consciousness are one in the same (if you exist.)

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Tue Sep 25, 2012 11:00 am Bagheera wrote: You keep using that word. I do not think it means what you think it means. Look, consciousness is not the be-all and end-all of reality. The world exists whether we perceive it or not. We cannot know the truth of much of anything, but that doesn't mean the existence of truth, or some iteration of it, is is dependent upon our perception thereof. In short it's not about us, and the origins of consciousness are quite mundane. There's not much to it beyond that.
